agent_reply: All project code under `src`, `lib`, and `inc` now uses `DBG_LOG*`; the only remaining `ESP_LOG*` references are inside the optional ESP-IDF backend in [dbg_log.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/dbg_log.h:1). I added `DBG_LOG_USE_SERIAL`, which routes logs through `Serial.printf` / `Serial.vprintf` and formats them like:
I also wired `platformio.ini` like this in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:75):

`CORE_DEBUG_LEVEL` and `LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL` stay as ESP-style values because framework headers like M5Unified read them before our `DBG_LOG_*` constants exist. Your project logging is controlled by `DBG_LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL`.

I checked the installed ESP32 SDK config and, yes, your suspicion looks right: the prebuilt package has `CONFIG_LOG_MAXIMUM_LEVEL=1`, so ESP-IDF logging above error is effectively capped. The new serial backend bypasses that.
Only the existing ESP-FTP-Server deprecated API warning showed up.

`CORE_DEBUG_LEVEL` and `LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL` stay as ESP-style values because framework headers like M5Unified read them before our `DBG_LOG_*` constants exist. Your project logging is controlled by `DBG_LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL`.
